nonBlockingIssues.description: Python line number references in Go comments are inaccurate (e.g., yj_property_info.go:833 says 'L276-278' but actual is L1126+). Only documentation comments affected, not code behavior.
Background-position/size condition checks only x variant instead of x OR y (yj_property_info.go:786,791). Works in practice since properties come in pairs from KFX data.
Link color resolution logic duplicated between linkClass() (storyline.go:2262) and applyAnnotations() (storyline.go:2662). Could drift over time.
Ruby annotation path ($757) skips style processing entirely. Python applies styles after ruby element creation. Low severity since ruby text typically doesn't carry CSS properties.
effectiveStyle uses shallow clone while Python uses deepcopy. Safe for current usage since annotation map is read-only, but could alias nested maps/slices if results are mutated.

create_opf has ~20+ Python branches with no Go counterpart (subject, rights, fixed_layout, orientation_lock, dictionary, magazine metadata, etc.). epub.Book struct lacks corresponding fields. Not triggered by current test books.
consolidate_html div-collapsing logic significantly simplified in Go vs Python. Go's shouldCollapseNestedDiv only handles simple nested-div case; Python checks parent tag types and body-level rules.
xmlns:epub detection via strings.Contains(section.BodyHTML, 'epub:type="') is heuristic; could miss single-quoted or namespace-prefixed variants. Works for all current test books.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: audit findings documents should use ✅ Parity (tested books only) or ⚠️ Parity (partial) instead of plain ✅ Parity when significant branches are unimplemented but not triggered by current test fixtures. This prevents future workers from assuming complete coverage.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: audit-epub-output-findings.md marks create_opf as '✅ Parity' with 'All 58 branches traced' despite ~20+ branches having no Go counterpart. audit-yj-to-epub-content audit documented 24 gaps as 'remaining' rather than as parity failures. Three reviews across content-pipeline milestone.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: workers should verify Python line numbers when adding cross-reference comments in Go code. Inaccurate line references (e.g., 'L276-278' when actual is L1126+) create confusion during future audits.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: audit-yj-to-epub-properties review found incorrect Python line numbers in yj_property_info.go:833 comments.

rejectedObservations.observation: prefixUniquePartOfSymbol regex behavior knowledge (underscores prevent double-prefixing)
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — this is implementation detail correctly matching Python; no action needed

rejectedObservations.observation: text_combine_in_use cross-module flag gap between properties and content modules
rejectedObservations.reason: valid gap but not actionable for scrutiny — this is a known deferred gap in the audit findings document, not a shared state issue

rejectedObservations.observation: Parity-auditor skill procedure ambiguity about documenting vs fixing all gaps
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — the skill procedure says to fix all gaps but a 1944-line audit producing 24 gaps is reasonable to document; this is a skill design question not a guidance issue
nice-to-have but not a guidance update; audit documents are discoverable via .factory/library/
